{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided, adrenaline (epinephrine) is used to control bleeding during surgery. Adrenaline works by constricting blood vessels and promoting vasoconstriction, which helps to reduce blood loss. However, adrenaline can also cause various side effects, including cardiac arrhythmias, which are irregular heartbeats. While it can lead to a drastic fall in blood pressure in some cases, this is not the primary concern when using adrenaline for controlling bleeding. Additionally, while an allergic reaction is a possibility, it is not the most common or expected side effect in this context. Therefore, the most likely side effect from using adrenaline to control bleeding during surgery is cardiac arrhythmias.", "answer_choice": "B"}
